---
title: "No Picutre when watching TV"
date: 2008-08-12
forum: Mythbuntu
---

### Post by tberman333 on 2008-08-12
I just setup Mythbuntu on top of an Ubuntu install.  I am trying to figure out how to configure the capture card so I can watch and record TV.

I have a very old capture card (it is a Hauppauge WinTV card).  I am unsure of the model number (but could find out more information on the card if needed.

I chose the Analog V4L as my capture card type and set the default input to Television (since my cable coax plugs directly into the card).  When I chose "Watch TV", I get sound but no picture.

As a note, I am able to watch TV using TV Time Television Viewer on Ubuntu (it just does not work through Mythbuntu).  

I am sure more information will be needed to help me out, but I am a newbie, so please just let me know what other information to give (and preferably how to find that info) and I will post it here.

Thanks for the help!

---

### Post by DemonBob on 2008-08-12
First and formost we need what type of card it is. 

Do a lspci -v from a terminal, and post the output. 

Next post your mythfrontend.log and mythbackend.log located in /var/log/mythtv

It's most likely going to be a setting missed somewhere, but this information will help us get and idea on whats happening.

---

### Post by DemonBob on 2008-08-12
> 2008-08-12 16:22:15.513 ChannelBase(3) Error: InitializeInputs(): 
			Could not get inputs for the capturecard.
			Perhaps you have forgotten to bind video
			sources to your card's inputs?


This seems it could be your issue, in mythtv-setup did you set up a video source, and input connections?

Also the card type is WinTV-Go, they are different variants of this card some with FM radio, some with out. This card does work with mythtv, infact it was the first card i purchased for my older pc. I later sold the entire media center to a friend and built a new one. So i know it works with some tinkering.

### Post by nigel502 on 2008-08-13
Probally would be best to setup it up as a mpeg2 capture card through mythbackend-setup

I think in the US the only option you have is schedules direct. It used to be donations based - zap2it, but open source people don't really donate that much and they had to rebrand and start charging . . .

### Post by tberman333 on 2008-08-22
Thanks for the help on this so far... I have been playing around with this a little more and still have had no luck.  

Here is what happens.  If I try to just watch TV, I get a black, then blue screen (with no picture).  There is sound though.  If I change the channel (using the number keys on my keyboard), the sound changes to the new channel, but still no picture.  If I then exit the MythTV front end, the sound keeps playing... the only way for me to turn off the sound is to either restart the machine, or turn on TV Time (to engage the capture card), then shut down TV time.  When I do this, I get a picture in TV time and also get sound for the channel that TV Time is set to.

I also tried recording a show.  When I did that, the recording shows in my recorded shows list (and shows a thumbnail of the video (so it seems the video can be played).  However, when I try to play it, I get no picture or sound (just blank screen again).  

I then tried to play the actaul file in var/lib/mythtv/recordings with MPlayer.  When I tried to open the .nuv file, I get picture, but there is no sound.  There is an error message in MPlayer that says "Cannot find codec for audio format 0x41574152"

It almost seems to me that MythTV does not know what player to use to watch tv or play recordings, and I am not sure where (or how) to set this up.

Thanks for the help!  And like I said in the first post, if there is more information that is needed, please let me know and I will post it here.

### Post by Chris Musampa on 2008-08-23
I don't know if its any hrlp to you but my old Acer laptop refused to play mythtv video after I added kubuntu desktop to a clean mythbuntu install, I found defaulting to gdm instead of kdm sorted it out.  Kde behaves no differently, just the login screen is different.
